subject: GeneralKnowledge
title: GeneralKnowledge Test - 08
description: A mix of fun and educational questions covering animals, geography, science, community, and health for Grade 4 students.

questions.q: What is the largest land animal in the world?
questions.explanation: The African elephant is the largest animal that lives on land.
questions.hint: It has large ears and a long trunk.
questions.objective: Identify common animals and their characteristics.

questions.q: Which ocean is located to the east of North America?
questions.explanation: The Atlantic Ocean separates North and South America from Europe and Africa.
questions.hint: It is the ocean you would cross to travel from New York to London.

questions.q: What is the boiling point of water in Celsius?
questions.explanation: At sea level, pure water boils at 100 degrees Celsius.
questions.objective: Understand basic scientific facts about temperature.

questions.q: Which part of the body do you use to pump blood?
questions.explanation: The heart acts as a pump to circulate blood throughout the entire body.
questions.hint: It is located in your chest.
questions.objective: Understand the function of major human body organs.

questions.q: What is the name of the imaginary line that runs around the middle of the Earth?
questions.explanation: The equator is a circle of latitude that divides the Earth into the Northern and Southern Hemispheres.

questions.q: Which of these is a form of precipitation?
questions.explanation: Precipitation is any form of water, liquid or solid, that falls from the atmosphere to the Earth's surface.

questions.q: Who is considered a community helper that keeps our teeth healthy?
questions.explanation: A dentist is a healthcare professional who focuses on oral health.
questions.hint: They tell you to brush and floss.

questions.q: What is the name of the star closest to Earth?
questions.explanation: The Sun is the closest star to the Earth, located at the center of our solar system.
questions.objective: Understand basic facts about the solar system.

questions.q: Which of these is a renewable energy source?
questions.explanation: Wind energy is renewable because the wind will always keep blowing.
questions.hint: It is used by turbines to create electricity.

questions.q: How many states are in the United States?
questions.explanation: There are 50 states that make up the United States of America.
questions.hint: It is a nice, round number.
